title: Introduction to assetIS
text: assetIS package provides importance sampling () estimators extreme tail probabilities ASSET subset-based meta-analysis statistic (Bhattacharjee et al., 2012). ASSET statistic ZASSET=maxA≠⌀|ZA|,Z_{\text{ASSET}} = \max_{\neq \varnothing} |Z_A|, maximum taken 2M−12^M - 1 non-empty subsets AA MM studies (cell types). Computing significance ZASSETZ_{\text{ASSET}} requires evaluating extreme tail probability, computationally prohibitive naive Monte Carlo p-value small. solves exponentially tilting simulation distribution toward region interest, achieving substantial variance reductions. package implements four estimators:
code: library(assetIS)

title: Introduction to assetIS
text: Consider M=5M = 5 independent studies moderate sample sizes. Gaussian appropriate zm∼N(0,1)z_m \sim N(0,1) null.
code: set.seed(42) M   <- 5 n   <- c(500, 600, 450, 700, 550)     # study sample sizes z   <- c(3.1, 2.9, 0.2, -0.1, 0.4)   # observed score statistics  ## Observed ASSET statistic b_obs <- max(abs(z)) cat("Observed ASSET statistic:", round(b_obs, 3), "\n") #> Observed ASSET statistic: 3.1

title: Introduction to assetIS
code: res_single <- asset_is_pvalue_ind(   z         = z,   n         = n,   b         = b_obs,   K         = 5000L,   two.sided = TRUE,   seed      = 1L ) print(res_single) #>     b      p.est         p.se    p.lower    p.upper  xi    K #> 1 3.1 0.02854922 0.0007158398 0.02714617 0.02995226 3.1 5000

title: Introduction to assetIS
text: b vector, single tilting parameter ξ=median(b)\xi = \text{median}(b) reused, making call expensive single threshold. p-value estimates grid thresholds (log scale). Shaded band 95% confidence interval.
code: b_grid <- seq(2.5, 5.5, by = 0.25)  res_range <- asset_is_pvalue_ind(   z         = z,   n         = n,   b         = b_grid,   K         = 1e4L,   two.sided = TRUE,   seed      = 2L ) head(res_range) #>      b       p.est         p.se     p.lower     p.upper xi     K #> 1 2.50 0.129859588 4.277290e-03 0.121476100 0.138243076  4 10000 #> 2 2.75 0.070631922 1.928003e-03 0.066853035 0.074410809  4 10000 #> 3 3.00 0.038349923 9.511407e-04 0.036485687 0.040214158  4 10000 #> 4 3.25 0.017220666 3.914875e-04 0.016453350 0.017987981  4 10000 #> 5 3.50 0.007671906 1.625664e-04 0.007353276 0.007990537  4 10000 #> 6 3.75 0.003107423 6.618144e-05 0.002977707 0.003237139  4 10000 plot(res_range$b, log10(res_range$p.est),      type = "l", lwd = 2,      xlab = "Threshold b", ylab = expression(log[10](hat(p))),      main = "Gaussian IS (independent studies)") polygon(c(res_range$b, rev(res_range$b)),         log10(c(res_range$p.upper, rev(res_range$p.lower))),         col = adjustcolor("steelblue", 0.2), border = NA) lines(res_range$b, log10(res_range$p.lower), lty = 2, col = "steelblue") lines(res_range$b, log10(res_range$p.upper), lty = 2, col = "steelblue")

title: Introduction to assetIS
text: case-control data, replace nmn_m effective sample size nmeff=nmϕm(1−ϕm)n_m^{\text{eff}} = n_m \phi_m(1-\phi_m), ϕm\phi_m case fraction study mm.
code: phi   <- c(0.30, 0.40, 0.50, 0.35, 0.45) n_eff <- n * phi * (1 - phi)  asset_is_pvalue_ind(   z         = z,   n         = n_eff,   b         = b_obs,   K         = 5000L,   two.sided = TRUE,   seed      = 3L ) #>     b      p.est         p.se    p.lower    p.upper  xi    K #> 1 3.1 0.02599386 0.0006764795 0.02466796 0.02731976 3.1 5000

title: Introduction to assetIS
text: studies share subjects, score statistics correlated known M×MM \times M correlation matrix Σ\Sigma.
code: rho   <- 0.35 Sigma <- outer(seq_len(M), seq_len(M), function(i, j) rho^abs(i - j))  res_corr <- asset_is_pvalue_corr(   z         = z,   n         = n,   Sigma     = Sigma,   b         = b_obs,   K         = 5000L,   two.sided = TRUE,   seed      = 4L ) print(res_corr) #>     b      p.est         p.se    p.lower   p.upper  xi    K #> 1 3.1 0.02008811 0.0005618315 0.01898692 0.0211893 3.1 5000

title: Introduction to assetIS
text: sample sizes small (e.g., single-cell eQTL, rare-disease meta-analysis), Gaussian approximation fails. non-Gaussian operates conditional observed phenotype matrix YY, simulating genotypes discrete Hardy–Weinberg distribution.

title: Introduction to assetIS
text: consider C=5C = 5 cell types, N=100N = 100 subjects, MAF f=0.15f = 0.15.
code: set.seed(42) N <- 100; C <- 5; f <- 0.15  ## Centered and scaled expression matrix Y <- scale(matrix(rnorm(N * C), N, C))  ## Simulate a genotype vector and compute the ASSET statistic g       <- rbinom(N, 2, f) sigma_g <- sqrt(2 * f * (1 - f)) z_eqtl  <- as.numeric(crossprod(Y, g - 2 * f)) / (sqrt(N) * sigma_g) b_eqtl  <- max(abs(z_eqtl)) cat("Observed ASSET (eQTL):", round(b_eqtl, 3), "\n") #> Observed ASSET (eQTL): 0.968  res_eqtl <- asset_is_nonnorm_ind(   Y         = Y,   f         = f,   b         = b_eqtl,   K         = 2000L,   two.sided = TRUE,   seed      = 5L ) print(res_eqtl) #>           b     p.est        p.se   p.lower   p.upper  b_anchor    K #> 1 0.9678839 0.9275846 0.007981053 0.9119417 0.9432275 0.9678839 2000

title: Introduction to assetIS
code: set.seed(7) N_total  <- 300 study_id <- sample(seq_len(C), N_total, replace = TRUE) phi_c    <- runif(C, 0.25, 0.40) y_bin    <- rbinom(N_total, 1, prob = phi_c[study_id])  ## Build centered outcome matrix Ycc <- matrix(0, N_total, C) for (i in seq_len(N_total))   Ycc[i, study_id[i]] <- y_bin[i] - phi_c[study_id[i]]  ## Compute score statistics using common MAF f = 0.2 f_cc    <- 0.20 sigma_g <- sqrt(2 * f_cc * (1 - f_cc)) g_cc    <- rbinom(N_total, 2, f_cc) z_cc    <- vapply(seq_len(C), function(c) {   idx <- which(study_id == c)   Nc  <- length(idx)   sum(Ycc[idx, c] * (g_cc[idx] - 2 * f_cc)) /     (sqrt(Nc * phi_c[c] * (1 - phi_c[c])) * sigma_g) }, numeric(1L)) b_cc <- max(abs(z_cc))  res_cc <- asset_is_nonnorm_ind(   Y         = Ycc,   f         = f_cc,   b         = b_cc,   study_id  = study_id,   K         = 2000L,   two.sided = TRUE,   seed      = 8L ) print(res_cc) #>        b     p.est        p.se  p.lower   p.upper b_anchor    K #> 1 1.8196 0.4290948 0.009828492 0.409831 0.4483586   1.8196 2000

title: Introduction to assetIS
text: Pass length-CC vector f handle different allele frequencies across ancestries.
code: f_multi <- c(0.15, 0.22, 0.10, 0.30, 0.18)  res_multi <- asset_is_nonnorm_ind(   Y         = Ycc,   f         = f_multi,   b         = b_cc,   study_id  = study_id,   K         = 2000L,   two.sided = TRUE,   seed      = 9L ) print(res_multi) #>        b     p.est       p.se   p.lower   p.upper b_anchor    K #> 1 1.8196 0.5387196 0.01145833 0.5162612 0.5611779   1.8196 2000

title: Introduction to assetIS
text: gene expression correlated across cell types, GLS-based estimator accounts empirical correlation Σ̂Y=Y⊤Y/N\widehat\Sigma_Y = Y^\top Y / N.
code: set.seed(42) N <- 150; C <- 5; f <- 0.15; rho <- 0.50  ## Correlated expression data if (requireNamespace("MASS", quietly = TRUE)) {   Sigma_expr <- (1 - rho) * diag(C) + rho   Y_corr     <- MASS::mvrnorm(N, mu = rep(0, C), Sigma = Sigma_expr) } else {   ## fallback: independent columns   Y_corr <- matrix(rnorm(N * C), N, C) } Y_corr <- scale(Y_corr)  g      <- rbinom(N, 2, f) sigma_g <- sqrt(2 * f * (1 - f))  ## GLS scores (approximate, using empirical Sigma_z) Sigma_z_hat <- crossprod(Y_corr) / N diag(Sigma_z_hat) <- 1 z_corr <- as.numeric(crossprod(Y_corr, g - 2 * f)) / (sqrt(N) * sigma_g) b_corr <- max(abs(z_corr))  res_nncorr <- asset_is_nonnorm_corr(   Y         = Y_corr,   f         = f,   b         = b_corr,   K         = 2000L,   two.sided = TRUE,   scale_Y   = FALSE,   # already scaled   seed      = 10L ) print(res_nncorr) #>          b     p.est       p.se   p.lower   p.upper b_anchor    K #> 1 1.394621 0.5139129 0.01145844 0.4914543 0.5363714 1.394621 2000

title: Introduction to assetIS
text: four functions support parallel execution via doParallel. Set parallel = TRUE optionally specify n.cores.
code: ## Not run in vignette (requires multiple cores) res_par <- asset_is_pvalue_ind(   z         = z,   n         = n,   b         = b_grid,   K         = 1e5L,   two.sided = TRUE,   parallel  = TRUE,   n.cores   = 4L,   seed      = 99L )

title: Introduction to assetIS
text: table gives approximate guidance number simulations KK needed achieve relative error roughly 10%. genome-wide significance (p≈5×10−8p \approx 5 \times 10^{-8}) recommend K≥105K \geq 10^5 Gaussian estimators K≥5×104K \geq 5 \times 10^4 non-Gaussian estimators modest CC (≤10\leq 10).

title: assetIS — Importance Sampling for ASSET
text: assetIS provides efficient importance sampling () estimators extreme tail probabilities ASSET subset-based meta-analysis statistic (Bhattacharjee et al., 2012). ASSET statistic defined ZASSET=maxA≠⌀|ZA|,Z_{\text{ASSET}} = \max_{\neq \varnothing} |Z_A|, maximum 2M−12^M - 1 non-empty subsets AA MM studies (cell types). Evaluating significance requires extreme tail probability, computationally intractable naive Monte Carlo. estimators assetIS use exponential tilting concentrate simulation effort target region, achieving orders--magnitude variance reductions.

title: assetIS — Importance Sampling for ASSET
text: four functions share design: One-sided two-sided p-values Scalar vector thresholds (efficient range approximation) Case-control studies (effective sample sizes binary outcome matrices) Multi-ancestry analyses study-specific allele frequencies Covariate adjustment (non-Gaussian estimators) Parallel computation via doParallel Reproducibility via master seed argument

title: assetIS — Importance Sampling for ASSET
text: Use Gaussian estimators (asset_is_pvalue_*) sample sizes large enough justify asymptotic normality study-level score statistics. Use non-Gaussian estimators (asset_is_nonnorm_*) : - Sample sizes small (e.g., single-cell eQTL across cell types) - trait rare (rare-disease meta-analysis) - wish condition observed phenotype data rather rely Gaussian approximation Use correlated variants (*_corr) : - Expression measurements correlated across cell types (eQTL) - Studies share subjects (overlapping case-control cohorts)

title: assetIS — Importance Sampling for ASSET
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("samuelanyaso/assetIS")

title: assetIS — Importance Sampling for ASSET
code: library(assetIS)  ## --- Gaussian IS, independent studies --- set.seed(1) M <- 5 n <- c(500, 600, 450, 700, 550) z <- c(3.1, 2.9, 0.2, -0.1, 0.4)  asset_is_pvalue_ind(z = z, n = n, b = max(abs(z)), K = 5000L)  ## --- Gaussian IS, correlated studies --- rho   <- 0.35 Sigma <- outer(1:M, 1:M, function(i, j) rho^abs(i - j)) asset_is_pvalue_corr(z = z, n = n, Sigma = Sigma, b = max(abs(z)), K = 5000L)  ## --- Non-Gaussian IS, single-cell eQTL --- N <- 100; C <- 5; f <- 0.15 Y <- scale(matrix(rnorm(N * C), N, C)) asset_is_nonnorm_ind(Y = Y, f = f, b = 4.5, K = 2000L)  ## --- Non-Gaussian IS, correlated cell types --- asset_is_nonnorm_corr(Y = Y, f = f, b = 4.5, K = 2000L)

title: assetIS — Importance Sampling for ASSET
code: ## Gaussian IS with effective sample sizes (case-control) phi   <- c(0.30, 0.40, 0.50, 0.35, 0.45) n_eff <- n * phi * (1 - phi) asset_is_pvalue_ind(z = z, n = n_eff, b = max(abs(z)), K = 5000L)  ## Non-Gaussian IS, multi-ancestry (study-specific MAF) f_multi  <- c(0.15, 0.22, 0.10, 0.30, 0.18) study_id <- sample(1:C, N, replace = TRUE) Ycc      <- matrix(0, N, C) # ... fill Ycc with centered binary outcomes ... asset_is_nonnorm_ind(Y = Ycc, f = f_multi, b = 4.5,                      study_id = study_id, K = 2000L)

title: assetIS — Importance Sampling for ASSET
code: asset_is_pvalue_ind(z = z, n = n, b = seq(3, 6, by = 0.1),                     K = 1e5L, parallel = TRUE, n.cores = 4L, seed = 42L)

title: assetIS — Importance Sampling for ASSET

what: assetIS: Importance Sampling for ASSET Tail Probability Estimation — assetIS-package
title: assetIS: Importance Sampling for ASSET Tail Probability Estimation — assetIS-package
text: assetIS package provides efficient importance sampling () estimators extreme tail probabilities ASSET subset-based meta-analysis statistic (Bhattacharjee et al., 2012).

title: assetIS: Importance Sampling for ASSET Tail Probability Estimation — assetIS-package
text: ASSET statistic defined $$Z_{\mathrm{ASSET}} = \max_{\neq \varnothing} |Z_A|,$$ maximum taken \(2^M - 1\) non-empty subsets \(\) \(M\) studies (cell types), \(Z_A\) fixed-effect meta-analysis statistic studies \(\). Evaluating significance \(Z_{\mathrm{ASSET}}\) requires estimating extreme tail probability, computationally prohibitive naive Monte Carlo. package implements exponential-tilting estimators achieve large variance reductions concentrating simulation effort target region. Four estimators provided, covering two design dimensions: Gaussian vs. non-Gaussian: Gaussian estimators (asset_is_pvalue_ind(), asset_is_pvalue_corr()) simulate study-level \(z\)-statistics (possibly correlated) normal distribution; appropriate sample sizes large enough justify asymptotic normality.  Non-Gaussian estimators (asset_is_nonnorm_ind(), asset_is_nonnorm_corr()) simulate genotypes directly trinomial (Hardy–Weinberg) distribution condition observed phenotype data \(Y\); designed small-sample settings single-cell eQTL rare-disease meta-analysis. Independent vs. correlated: Independent estimators (asset_is_pvalue_ind(), asset_is_nonnorm_ind()) assume score statistics uncorrelated across studies / cell types.  Correlated estimators (asset_is_pvalue_corr(), asset_is_nonnorm_corr()) account inter-study correlation via known estimated correlation matrix. four functions share common interface support: One-sided two-sided p-values. Scalar vector thresholds \(b\) (range approximation vectors, reusing single tilting parameter neighbourhood). Case-control studies (effective sample sizes binary outcome matrices). Multi-ancestry analyses study-specific allele frequencies. Covariate adjustment (non-Gaussian estimators). Optional parallel computation via doParallel. Reproducibility via master seed argument.

title: assetIS: Importance Sampling for ASSET Tail Probability Estimation — assetIS-package
text: Bhattacharjee S, Rajaraman P, Bhattacharjee S, et al. (2012). subset-based approach improves power interpretation combined analysis genetic association studies heterogeneous traits. American Journal Human Genetics, 90(5), 821–835. Shi J Siegmund D (2007). Importance sampling estimating p-values linkage analysis. Biostatistics, 8(3), 587–600. Siegmund D (1976). Importance sampling Monte Carlo study sequential tests. Annals Statistics, 4(4), 673–684.

title: assetIS: Importance Sampling for ASSET Tail Probability Estimation — assetIS-package
text: Maintainer: Samuel Anyaso-Samuel samuel.anyaso-samuel@nih.gov

what: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian Settings with Correlated Score Statistics — asset_is_nonnorm_corr
title: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian Settings with Correlated Score Statistics — asset_is_nonnorm_corr
text: Estimates \(p(b \mid Y) = P_0(\max_A Z_A > b \mid Y)\) (one-sided) \(p(b \mid Y) = P_0(\max_A |Z_A| > b \mid Y)\) (two-sided) normality assumption may fail score statistics correlated, e.g., due correlated gene expression across cell types overlapping subjects case-control meta-analysis. conditional covariance score vector \(\mathbf{z}\) estimated \(\widehat\Sigma_Y = Y^\top Y / N\) (scaling). optimal GLS statistic subset \(\) $$Z_A = \frac{\mathbf{1}_A^\top \Sigma_A^{-1} \mathbf{z}_A}             {\sqrt{\mathbf{1}_A^\top \Sigma_A^{-1} \mathbf{1}_A}},$$ admits linear genotype representation \(Z_A = \sum_i \omega_{,}(g_i - \tilde g_i)\) GLS-adjusted weights. algorithm follows asset_is_nonnorm_ind(): Newton solve \(\xi_A\), trinomial genotype simulation tilted HWE distribution, mixture weight \((2^C-1) / \sum_A \exp\{\xi_A Z_A - \phi_A(\xi_A)\}\).

title: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian Settings with Correlated Score Statistics — asset_is_nonnorm_corr
code: asset_is_nonnorm_corr(   Y,   f,   b,   study_id = NULL,   g_center = NULL,   tau = NULL,   Sigma_z = NULL,   scale_Y = TRUE,   ridge = 1e-10,   two.sided = TRUE,   K = 5000L,   b_anchor = NULL,   newton_tol = 1e-10,   newton_max_iter = 100L,   parallel = FALSE,   n.cores = parallel::detectCores() - 1L,   seed = NULL )

title: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian Settings with Correlated Score Statistics — asset_is_nonnorm_corr
text: Y Numeric \(N \times C\) matrix. See asset_is_nonnorm_ind() mode-specific conventions. Scaled internally scale_Y = TRUE. f Numeric scalar length-\(C\) vector.  Minor allele frequency (MAF).  scalar applies common MAF cell types / studies; vector applies study-specific MAFs (multi-ancestry case-control).  values must \((0, 0.5]\). b Numeric scalar vector threshold values. study_id Integer vector length \(N\). Activates case-control mode. Default NULL. g_center Numeric vector length \(N\). Per-subject genotype centering. Default NULL uses \(2f_{c_i}\). tau Numeric positive vector length \(C\). Scale factors covariate-adjusted eQTL. Default NULL. Sigma_z Numeric \(C \times C\) matrix.  Conditional covariance (correlation) matrix \((z_1,\ldots,z_C)\) given \(Y\). Defaults NULL, case \(\widehat{\Sigma}_Y =   Y^\top Y / N\) computed internally scaling.  Supply pre-computed externally estimated correlation matrix override default (e.g., previous run, estimated larger reference panel).  Must symmetric positive definite unit diagonal. scale_Y Logical.  TRUE (default), Y centered scaled mean zero unit variance per column.  Set FALSE Y already pre-processed. ridge Positive numeric. Ridge added \(\Sigma_A\) inversion. Default 1e-10. two.sided Logical. Default TRUE. K Positive integer. Number simulations. Default 5000L. b_anchor Numeric scalar.  anchor threshold \(b_0\) \(\xi_{,\pm}\) solved.  Defaults NULL, using \(b_0 = \mathrm{median}(b)\).  Ignored scalar b. newton_tol Numeric. Newton tolerance. Default 1e-10. newton_max_iter Integer. Maximum Newton iterations. Default 100L. parallel Logical. Default FALSE. n.cores Positive integer. Default parallel::detectCores()-1. seed Integer NULL. Default NULL.

title: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian Settings with Correlated Score Statistics — asset_is_nonnorm_corr
text: data frame one row per threshold b columns: b Threshold value supplied. p.est point estimate \(p(b \mid Y)\). p.se Estimated standard error p.est. p.lower Lower bound 95\ \([0,1]\). p.upper Upper bound 95\ \([0,1]\). b_anchor Anchor threshold used Newton solve. K Number simulations used.

title: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian Settings with Correlated Score Statistics — asset_is_nonnorm_corr
text: Conditional ASSET Normality Fails: Correlated Cell Types Overlapping Studies Covariate adjustment.  tau g_center supplied, adjusted omega formula \(\omega_{,} = N^{-1/2}\sum_{c \}a_{c,}\,y'_{ic}/\tau_c / \sqrt{\mathbf{1}_A^\top a_A}\) used, \(y'_{ic}\) expression residuals regressing covariates. Scaling. formula \(\widehat\Sigma_Y = Y^\top Y/N\) requires zero-mean, unit-variance columns. Set scale_Y = TRUE (default) apply base::scale() internally. Supplying Sigma_z. pre-computed reference-panel correlation matrix can passed directly via Sigma_z, bypassing empirical estimate. Modes. three analysis modes asset_is_nonnorm_ind() supported (eQTL, covariate-adjusted eQTL, case-control), now GLS weights derived \(\widehat\Sigma_Y\).

title: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian Settings with Correlated Score Statistics — asset_is_nonnorm_corr
text: Bhattacharjee, S. et al. (2012). subset-based approach improves power interpretation combined analysis genetic association studies heterogeneous traits. American Journal Human Genetics, 90(5), 821–835. Shi, J. et al. (2007). Importance sampling estimating p-values linkage analysis. Journal American Statistical Association, 102(479), 929–937. Siegmund, D. (1976). Importance sampling Monte Carlo study sequential tests. Annals Statistics, 4(4), 673–684.

title: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian Settings with Correlated Score Statistics — asset_is_nonnorm_corr

what: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian, Independent Settings — asset_is_nonnorm_ind
title: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian, Independent Settings — asset_is_nonnorm_ind
text: Estimates conditional tail probability \(p(b \mid Y) = P_0(\max_A Z_A > b \mid Y)\) (one-sided) \(p(b \mid Y) = P_0(\max_A |Z_A| > b \mid Y)\) (two-sided) ASSET statistic normality assumption score statistics may violated cell types / studies independent. arises single-cell eQTL mapping small sample sizes meta-analysis rare diseases. Inference conditional observed phenotype matrix \(Y\); source randomness genotype vector \((g_1,\ldots,g_N)\). Genotypes simulated discrete (trinomial) distribution Hardy–Weinberg equilibrium (HWE), per-subject exponential tilting. tilting parameter \(\xi_A\) subset chosen satisfy \(\phi_A'(\xi_A) = b_0\) via Newton's method. function supports three analysis modes selected automatically: eQTL \(N\) subjects \(C\) cell types (study_id = NULL, tau = NULL). eQTL covariate adjustment tau g_center supplied; Y contains expression residuals. Case-control meta-analysis study_id supplied; subjects nested within \(C\) studies; multi-ancestry via length-\(C\) vector f.

title: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian, Independent Settings — asset_is_nonnorm_ind
code: asset_is_nonnorm_ind(   Y,   f,   b,   study_id = NULL,   g_center = NULL,   tau = NULL,   scale_Y = TRUE,   two.sided = TRUE,   K = 5000L,   b_anchor = NULL,   newton_tol = 1e-10,   newton_max_iter = 100L,   parallel = FALSE,   n.cores = parallel::detectCores() - 1L,   seed = NULL )

title: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian, Independent Settings — asset_is_nonnorm_ind
text: Y Numeric matrix dimension \(N \times C\), centered scaled (mean zero, unit variance per column) raw (scale_Y = TRUE).  eQTL: expression matrix (residual matrix covariate adjustment, tau supplied). case-control: matrix whose entry Y[,   study_id[]] contains centered binary outcome \(y_{ic} - \bar{y}_c\) (covariate residual \(y_{ic} - \hat{\mu}_{ic}\)) subject \(\); entries columns ignored. f Numeric scalar length-\(C\) vector. Minor allele frequency (MAF). Scalar common MAF; length-\(C\) vector study-specific MAFs (multi-ancestry case-control). Must \((0, 0.5]\). b Numeric scalar vector threshold values. vector, \(\xi_A\) solved anchor \(b_0 = \mathrm{median}(\mathtt{b})\) (b_anchor). study_id Integer vector length \(N\) values \(\{1,\ldots,C\}\). Activates case-control mode. Default NULL (eQTL mode). g_center Numeric vector length \(N\). Per-subject genotype centering values \(\tilde g_i\). Default NULL uses \(2f_{c_i}\). tau Numeric positive vector length \(C\). Per-cell-type scale factors covariate-adjusted eQTL weights. Default NULL. scale_Y Logical.  TRUE (default), Y centered scaled mean zero unit variance per column.  Set FALSE Y already pre-processed. two.sided Logical. Default TRUE. K Positive integer. Number simulations. Default 5000L. b_anchor Numeric scalar.  anchor threshold \(b_0\) \(\xi_{,\pm}\) solved.  Defaults NULL, using \(b_0 = \mathrm{median}(b)\).  Ignored scalar b. newton_tol Positive numeric. Newton convergence tolerance. Default 1e-10. newton_max_iter Positive integer. Maximum Newton iterations. Default 100L. parallel Logical. Default FALSE. n.cores Positive integer. Default parallel::detectCores()-1. seed Integer NULL. Default NULL.

title: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian, Independent Settings — asset_is_nonnorm_ind

K Number simulations used.

title: Conditional IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Non-Gaussian, Independent Settings — asset_is_nonnorm_ind
text: Conditional ASSET Normality Fails: Independent Cell Types Studies Two-sided test.  proper two-sided mixture tilt used rather heuristic halving threshold doubling estimate. CGF \(\phi_A(\xi)\) symmetric \(\xi\) general (data-dependent) weights \(\omega_{,}\), two per-subset tilting parameters solved: \(\xi_{,+}\) satisfying \(\phi_A'(\xi_{,+}) = +b_0\) (tilting toward upper tail) \(\xi_{,-}\) satisfying \(\phi_A'(\xi_{,-}) = -b_0\) (tilting toward lower tail). simulation, driving subset \(A_k\) sign \(s_k \\{+1,-1\}\) chosen uniformly random.  Genotypes drawn tilted distribution corresponding \(\xi_{A_k,s_k}\), weight denominator involves \(2(2^C-1)\) terms: $$\frac{2(2^C-1)}{\sum_A \left[   \exp\{\xi_{,+} Z_A - \phi_A(\xi_{,+})\} +   \exp\{\xi_{,-} Z_A - \phi_A(\xi_{,-})\} \right]} \mathcal{}\!\left\{\max_A |Z_A| > b\right\}.$$ Range thresholds.  b vector, \(\xi_{,\pm}\) solved anchor \(b_0 = \mathrm{median}(b)\) (b_anchor) original scale \(b\). Scaling \(Y\).  column \(Y\) mean zero unit variance.  scale_Y = TRUE (default), function applies base::scale \(Y\) internally.

what: Gaussian IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Correlated or Overlapping Studies — asset_is_pvalue_corr
title: Gaussian IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Correlated or Overlapping Studies — asset_is_pvalue_corr
text: Estimates \(p(b) = P_0(\max_A Z_A > b)\) (one-sided) \(p(b) = P_0(\max_A |Z_A| > b)\) (two-sided) ASSET statistic study-level score statistics \((z_1,\ldots,z_M)\) correlated, e.g., due overlapping subjects across studies. optimal meta-analysis statistic subset \(\) $$Z_A = \frac{N_A^\top \Sigma_A^{-1} z_A}             {\sqrt{N_A^\top \Sigma_A^{-1} N_A}},$$ \(N_A = (\sqrt{n_m})_{m \}\) \(\Sigma_A\) principal submatrix \(M \times M\) inter-study correlation matrix \(\Sigma\). algorithm tilts joint MVN distribution via \(dP_{\xi,} = \exp(\xi Z_A - \xi^2/2)\,dP_0\).

title: Gaussian IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Correlated or Overlapping Studies — asset_is_pvalue_corr
code: asset_is_pvalue_corr(   z,   n,   Sigma,   b,   K = 5000L,   two.sided = TRUE,   xi = NULL,   parallel = FALSE,   n.cores = parallel::detectCores() - 1L,   seed = NULL )

title: Gaussian IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Correlated or Overlapping Studies — asset_is_pvalue_corr
text: z Numeric vector length \(M\). Observed study-level score statistics. n Numeric vector length \(M\). Study sample sizes. case-control studies supply effective sample sizes \(n_m^{\mathrm{eff}} = n_m \phi_m (1-\phi_m)\). Sigma Numeric \(M \times M\) inter-study correlation matrix null. Must symmetric positive definite unit diagonal. b Numeric scalar vector threshold values. K Positive integer. Number simulations. Default 5000L. two.sided Logical. TRUE (default) compute two-sided p-value. xi Numeric scalar. Tilting parameter. Defaults median(b) (two-sided halving). parallel Logical. Default FALSE. n.cores Positive integer. Default parallel::detectCores()-1. seed Integer NULL. Default NULL.

K Number simulations used.

title: Gaussian IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Correlated or Overlapping Studies — asset_is_pvalue_corr
text: Importance Sampling ASSET: Correlated Studies, Gaussian Approximation Case-control studies. Replace \(n_m\) effective sample size \(n_m^{\mathrm{eff}} = n_m \phi_m (1-\phi_m)\). Numerical stability. \(\Sigma\) numerically positive definite, function attempts regularisation via Matrix::nearPD followed diagonal jitter, issuing warning applied. independent studies use asset_is_pvalue_ind(). non-Gaussian settings use asset_is_nonnorm_ind() asset_is_nonnorm_corr().

what: Gaussian IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Independent Studies — asset_is_pvalue_ind
title: Gaussian IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Independent Studies — asset_is_pvalue_ind
text: Estimates \(p(b) = P_0(\max_A Z_A > b)\) (one-sided) \(p(b) = P_0(\max_A |Z_A| > b)\) (two-sided) ASSET statistic \(M\) study-level score statistics \((z_1,\ldots,z_M)\) independent approximately normally distributed global null. algorithm uses exponential tilting joint Gaussian distribution toward target region, substantially reducing variance compared naive Monte Carlo extreme thresholds. non-empty subset \(\subseteq \{1,\ldots,M\}\), fixed-effect meta-analysis statistic $$Z_A = \frac{\sum_{m \} \sqrt{n_m}\, z_m}{\sqrt{\sum_{l \} n_l}}.$$ estimator based likelihood-ratio identity $$P_0(\max_A Z_A > b) = \frac{1}{2^M-1} \sum_{A_0}   E_{\xi,A_0}\!\left(     \frac{2^M-1}{\sum_A \exp\{\xi Z_A - \xi^2/2\}}     \,\mathcal{}\{\max_A Z_A > b\}   \right),$$ near-optimal choice \(\xi = \mathrm{median}(\mathtt{b})\).

title: Gaussian IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Independent Studies — asset_is_pvalue_ind
code: asset_is_pvalue_ind(   z,   n,   b,   K = 5000L,   two.sided = TRUE,   xi = NULL,   parallel = FALSE,   n.cores = parallel::detectCores() - 1L,   seed = NULL )

title: Gaussian IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Independent Studies — asset_is_pvalue_ind
text: z Numeric vector length \(M\). Observed study-level score statistics, approximately \(N(0,1)\) global null. n Numeric vector length \(M\). Study sample sizes. case-control studies supply effective sample sizes \(n_m^{\mathrm{eff}} = n_m \phi_m (1-\phi_m)\). b Numeric scalar vector threshold values. vector, single \(\xi = \mathrm{median}(\mathtt{b})\) reused across thresholds. K Positive integer. Number simulations. Default 5000L. two.sided Logical. TRUE (default) compute \(P_0(\max_A |Z_A| > b)\); FALSE compute \(P_0(\max_A Z_A > b)\). xi Numeric scalar. Tilting parameter. Defaults median(b) (two-sided halving). Override supply custom value. parallel Logical. TRUE distribute simulations across n.cores workers. Default FALSE. n.cores Positive integer. Workers used parallel = TRUE. Default parallel::detectCores() - 1. seed Integer NULL. Master random seed. Default NULL.

title: Gaussian IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Independent Studies — asset_is_pvalue_ind
text: data frame one row per threshold value b following columns: b threshold value. p.est point estimate \(p(b)\). p.se Estimated standard error p.est. p.lower Lower bound 95\ \(\hat{p} - 1.96\,\widehat{\mathrm{SE}}\), truncated \([0,1]\). p.upper Upper bound 95\ truncated \([0,1]\). xi Tilting parameter used corresponding threshold. K Number simulations used.

title: Gaussian IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Independent Studies — asset_is_pvalue_ind
text: Importance Sampling ASSET: Independent Studies, Gaussian Approximation Case-control studies. Replace \(n_m\) effective sample size \(n_m^{\mathrm{eff}} = n_m \phi_m (1-\phi_m)\), \(\phi_m\) fraction cases study \(m\). Range thresholds. b vector, single tilting parameter \(\xi = \mathrm{median}(\mathtt{b})\) shared, thresholds evaluated one pass via findInterval. Two-sided test. Uses symmetry \(P_0(\max_A |Z_A| > b) = 2 P_0(\max_A Z_A > b/2)\). Parallel computation.  parallel = TRUE simulations distributed across n.cores workers using foreach doParallel backend. correlated studies use asset_is_pvalue_corr(). non-Gaussian settings use asset_is_nonnorm_ind() asset_is_nonnorm_corr().

title: Gaussian IS Estimator of ASSET Tail Probabilities for Independent Studies — asset_is_pvalue_ind
assetIS 0.1.0
text: asset_is_pvalue_ind(): Gaussian ASSET statistic assuming independent studies asymptotic normality score statistics. asset_is_pvalue_corr(): Gaussian ASSET statistic study-level score statistics correlated (e.g., due overlapping subjects). asset_is_nonnorm_ind(): Conditional ASSET statistic non-Gaussian settings (single-cell eQTL, rare-disease meta-analysis) assuming independent cell types studies. Genotypes simulated discrete Hardy–Weinberg distribution conditional observed phenotype matrix. Supports eQTL, covariate-adjusted eQTL, case-control modes, common study-specific allele frequencies. asset_is_nonnorm_corr(): Conditional ASSET statistic non-Gaussian settings correlated score statistics. GLS weights derived empirical conditional covariance Σ̂Y=Y⊤Y/N\widehat\Sigma_Y = Y^\top Y / N replace equal-weight formula.

what: Features shared across all four functions
title: assetIS 0.1.0
text: One-sided two-sided p-values. Scalar vector thresholds range approximation (single tilting parameter reused neighbourhood thresholds). Case-control support via effective sample sizes (Gaussian estimators) binary outcome matrices (non-Gaussian estimators). Multi-ancestry analyses study-specific allele frequencies (non-Gaussian estimators). Covariate adjustment (non-Gaussian estimators). Optional parallel computation via doParallel. Reproducibility via master seed argument per-worker derived seeds. 95% Wald confidence intervals p-value estimates.
